narrative.headline: 2019 Subaru Forester: cracked windshields lead the complaints, with electrical and engine issues close behind
narrative.summary: The dominant complaint pattern centers on windshield cracking that owners report discovering spontaneously, without apparent impact. Beyond the glass, owners also describe sudden, complete electrical shutoffs mid-turn and an engine hesitation-and-lurch behavior under light throttle input. A smaller share of reports involves the forward collision avoidance system failing to brake and instead accelerating into obstacles.

issues.title: Visibility/Wiper
issues.blurb: Owners report windshields cracking on their own while the vehicle is parked or in normal use, with no apparent impact event.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Owners report the entire electrical system shutting off without warning while turning, taking out power steering and emergency braking simultaneously. · 7 fire · 7 crash · 1 injured

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Owners report the engine hesitating and then lurching forward when the gas pedal is pressed lightly at low to moderate speeds. · 7 fire · 7 crash

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: Owners report the system failing to brake as expected and instead allowing the vehicle to lurch forward into another vehicle. · 2 crash

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 38 reports (5%) of surging or jerking at speed. Linked to 5 crashes and 1 injury, so have drivetrain symptoms checked early.
